paragraphs: Tech Mahindra SMART Academy for Healthcare, Delhi admission 2025is entrance-based. Students are offered a variety of UG Diploma, Certificate, and PG Diploma programmes in the field of Healthcare. Admission to the courses is made based on an institute-level entrance test followed by PI. Tech Mahindra SMART Academy for Healthcare, Delhi accepts candidates who fulfil the admission criteria of the offered course. The institute is associated withHarijan Sevak Sangh.Interested students can read the following article to know all about the courses, eligibility, selection criteria, and admission process forTech Mahindra SMART Academy for Healthcare, Delhi.

Tech Mahindra SMART Academy for Healthcare, Delhi course admissionis subject to clearance of the admission criteria set by the institute. For admission into the undergraduate programmes, students need to pass Class 12 or equivalent from a recognised board. Further, for PG admissions, students need to complete graduation. The below-mentioned table contains information regarding the eligibility & selection criteria forTech Mahindra SMART Academy for Healthcare, Delhi courses:

The admission procedure at Tech Mahindra SMART Academy for Healthcare, Delhi begins with registration followed by an in-house entrance followed by Personal Interview. Admission to the various programmes offered at the institute is currently open. Interested students can follow the steps listed below to completeTech Mahindra SMART Academy for Healthcare Delhi admission process:
Step 1: Application Process
Aspirants fulfilling the eligibility requirements can contact the college for the application form. Aspirants can also visit the campus to fill out the application form.
Step 2: Selection Process

Applicants need to take the in-house entrance test based on which the admission counsellors will guide students for the course that befits them the most. The test will contain a standard school science syllabus. Those who clear the entrance test are required to appear for the Personal Interview conducted by a senior member of the faculty.
Step 3: Admission Confirmation
Selected students need to provide the required documents and pay the first instalment ofTech Mahindra SMART Academy for Healthcare, Delhi feesfor seat confirmation.

college_info.college_name: Tech Mahindra SMART Academy for Healthcare, Delhi Scholarship Details

paragraphs: Yes, Tech Mahindra SMART Academy for Healthcare, Delhi offers scholarhsips. The institute offers scholarships to deserving candidates under various scholarship schemes. The institute aims to support needy youth in making a career in the healthcare sector. The below-mentioned are Tech Mahindra SMART Academy for Healthcare, Delhi scholarships:

Tech Mahindra SMART Academy for Healthcare, Delhi offers several scholarship schemes to students. Students can apply for Pulse and Smart Academy Scholarships to students. Below are the types of scholarships offered by the institute along with their criteria:
Upto 50% of the tuition fee is waived on achieving above 90% marks in the Annual Exam and 90% attendance in the first year of Diploma courses
Up to 50% of the tuition fee is waived off on achieving above 90% marks in the Half-yearly Exam and 90% attendance in H1 in one-year courses.
Up to 80% of the total fee is waived off and the complete Hostel Fees are waived off.
Besides, the academy also offers a Scholarship for Cancer Survivors and Dependents of Cancer Victims/Survivors and a Scholarship for Children of Anganwadi/ASHA/Sanitation Workers.

Tech Mahindra SMART Academy for Healthcare, Delhi  has affordable fee strcture. The fee ranges between INR 60,000- INR 1 lakh. The fee strcture include various components such as tuition fees, one-time payment, hostel, and others. The fee components vary from course to course. Candidates are offered various facilities during their campus programmes and some of these components are paid once and a few are paid annually as per the course duration.
tables: Upto 50% of the tuition fee is waived on achieving above 90% marks in the Annual Exam and 90% attendance in the first year of Diploma coursesUp to 50% of the tuition fee is waived off on achieving above 90% marks in the Half-yearly Exam and 90% attendance in H1 in one-year courses.
Scholarship for students from priority States/UT (Jammu, Kashmir, Ladakh, North Eastern States, Himachal Pradesh)
Applicable only for healthcare courses above or equivalent to 1-year durationUp to 80% of the total fee is waived off and the complete Hostel Fees are waived off.
As per the scholarship, up to 50% of the total fees are waived off

reviews_list.batch_info: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2028
reviews_list.review_title: Faculty members are good, but hostel rooms need to be increase a lot .

review_content.Placements: Our college has a very good environment for studying, as it provides two huge libraries, one of which has AC. The hostel is also good, but not the best. Patient flow is moderate but less than at 5 colleges in Kolkata. The advantage is that it is in the suburbs of Kolkata, so you will be well connected to the metropolitan area. Professors are good and often give punishments, like in school, if homework is not done. Bad news for those who bunk classes: it strictly enforces a 75% attendance requirement, so be regular. And before coming for MBBS, keep your prestige at home; otherwise, it will be thrown out by the professors during viva. During the 4.5 years, there are countless vivas.

review_content.Placements: 125 students were admitted. The highest package was not mentioned, but all of them will get a 1-year internship where they will receive 4.2 LPA. West Bengal University of Health Sciences offers internships for 1 year. Doctors in MBBS, MD, and DNB also offer nursing.
review_content.Infrastructure: Above average, at first you have to stay in dorms for 1 month with your batchmates, and then the authority will allot you a specific room where you have to share it with your 2 batchmates, and then comes the food, which is average 24 food is not available.

review_summary.college_name: College of Medicine and Sagore Dutta Hospital Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2028
individual_reviews.title: Overall, the college is really good.

content.Placements: Since it's a government college, all undergraduate students get to do a well-paid internship for one year after completing their four and a half years of studying. Even after that, we can easily apply to become a house staff member in a particular department.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure of the college is good in general, as there are big classrooms and a lecture theatre. We have QC libraries as well. A slight improvement if made for hostel infrastructure will be even better, like bathrooms and toilets. The food quality of the canteen can be improved a little bit.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2029
individual_reviews.title: Pros and Cons of College of Medicine and Sagore Dutta Hospital.

content.Placements: There is nothing called placement in MBBS. There is a compulsory 1-year rotatory internship for which currently approximately Rs. 30,000 is paid, which keeps increasing every year. After that, you may join the college itself as a Housestaff or an RMO at any hospital. But no doctor is unemployed.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure of the college is pretty good. There is a huge library with AC and non-AC reading rooms. Labs are there for each department with optimum microscopes and the other needed stuff. Classrooms are huge. 1st-year students are generally given non-AC lecture theatres. The hostel is pretty average. It is clean, and every hostel has its own mess on the ground floor. There is a common room with table tennis and other games. There are sports tournaments for cricket, badminton, table tennis, and many others, but College of Medicine and Sagore Dutta Hospital don't have a playground.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2028
individual_reviews.title: Faculty members are good, but hostel rooms need to be increase a lot .

content.Placements: Our college has a very good environment for studying, as it provides two huge libraries, one of which has AC. The hostel is also good, but not the best. Patient flow is moderate but less than at 5 colleges in Kolkata. The advantage is that it is in the suburbs of Kolkata, so you will be well connected to the metropolitan area. Professors are good and often give punishments, like in school, if homework is not done. Bad news for those who bunk classes: it strictly enforces a 75% attendance requirement, so be regular. And before coming for MBBS, keep your prestige at home; otherwise, it will be thrown out by the professors during viva. During the 4.5 years, there are countless vivas.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2029
individual_reviews.title: Review of college of medicine and sagoredutta hospital by a student of 2 St proff MBBS.

content.Placements: 125 students were admitted. The highest package was not mentioned, but all of them will get a 1-year internship where they will receive 4.2 LPA. West Bengal University of Health Sciences offers internships for 1 year. Doctors in MBBS, MD, and DNB also offer nursing.
content.Infrastructure: Above average, at first you have to stay in dorms for 1 month with your batchmates, and then the authority will allot you a specific room where you have to share it with your 2 batchmates, and then comes the food, which is average 24 food is not available.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2022
individual_reviews.title: Review of Sagar Dutta Medical College And Hospital, Kolkata.

content.Placements: We get an internship for a year. Then, it is on us what we want to do, either to opt for further studies or work in various hospitals and medical institutions. We can also take up a private practice. The medical field is not about jobs, more about serving people and treating. So, if we are ready to serve, then we find ways to. During internships in college, it is rotatory. We get experience from all major fields of medical sciences. We get exposed to rural areas in community medicine.
content.Infrastructure: We have a 500 bedded multi-speciality hospital. As it is a government setup, we get all facilities at a nominal price. We have a central library as well as departmental libraries. We do have state-of-the-art labs for para-clinical subjects. There are well-maintained labs for pre-clinical ones. Not to mention the hospital for the clinical practice. We even have a very busy OPD schedule.
